methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series C → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.7x) to $250M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 3 same-sector + same-stage peers in Biotech & Health. Range: $1.5B - $4.0B.

methods.name: Public-Comp Anchoring
methods.skip_reason: target revenue not on file (private companies rarely disclose)
methods.rationale: Public Biotech & Health P/S median is 6.6x across 3 tickers. Cannot apply without target's revenue.

private.name: Ultima Genomics
private.rationale: Same sector (Biotech & Health), same stage (Series C), post-money $4B

private.name: Insitro
private.rationale: Same sector (Biotech & Health), same stage (Series C), post-money $2.4B

private.name: Colossal Biosciences
private.rationale: Same sector (Biotech & Health), same stage (Series C), post-money $1.5B

data.narrative:
eGenesis is a Series C-stage biotech company founded by Luhan Yang and George Church, having raised $250M+ to date. The valuation analysis draws on a peer universe of 3 public-market biotech comparables (RXRX, TEM, ONT.L) and 8 private-sector peers, with particular focus on 3 same-stage, same-sector companies: Ultima Genomics ($4.0B), Insitro ($2.4B), and Colossal Biosciences ($1.5B).

Two triangulation methods are applicable. Stage-progression analysis applies a 1.7x step-up multiplier to the $250M raised proxy, implying a valuation of $425M. Peer-median benchmarking across the three same-sector, same-stage comparables yields $2.4B, with a range spanning $1.5B to $4.0B. Public-market anchoring via price-to-sales multiples (sector median 6.6x) cannot be applied due to absence of disclosed revenue figures. The two applicable methods diverge meaningfully, reflecting the wide variance in Series C biotech valuations.

Synthesizing both approaches yields a triangulated range of $361M (low), $1.4B (median), and $2.8B (high). The wide spread reflects uncertainty around eGenesis's stage-specific position within the peer cohort—specifically, whether it tracks closer to Colossal's $1.5B valuation or approaches Ultima Genomics' $4.0B. Key drivers of final valuation within this range will be IP defensibility, clinical progress velocity, and capital efficiency relative to peer trajectories.
